codeReview.summary: Fix is correct and minimal. Single-line rename of handleDiscoverySetupDeepLink at app.js line 5145 restores the function that runPostAccessBootstrapOnce() calls, reversing the accidental renaming from commit 0d2b092. The function correctly reads window.location.search directly (no url/context params needed). All 6 cold-start handoff tests pass. All repo validators (typecheck, lint, full test suite of 272 tests) pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Two similarly-named functions exist in app.js for different purposes: handleDiscoverySetupDeepLink() (line 5145, reads window.location.search for ?setup=discovery deep links) and handleDiscoveryWizardVerification(url, context) (line 4192, handles webhook verification). The accidental renaming in commit 0d2b092 suggests these names are close enough to cause confusion. A naming convention (e.g., suffix pattern like DeepLink vs Verification) or cross-reference comment in app.js would prevent future name collisions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: grep shows handleDiscoverySetupDeepLink at line 5145, handleDiscoveryWizardVerification at line 4192. runPostAccessBootstrapOnce calls handleDiscoverySetupDeepLink() at line 5160. The 0d2b092 diff shows the original handleDiscoverySetupDeepLink was replaced during the run-status tracker feature, breaking the bootstrap call.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature description mentions 'add deterministic automated checks for run-status tracker transitions' but no new test file was added. The existing tests/discovery-cold-start-handoffs.test.mjs suite covers the deep-link deferral contract (handleDiscoverySetupDeepLink, runPostAccessBootstrapOnce, requestDiscoverySetup, resumePendingDiscoverySetupIfNeeded) but does NOT exercise DiscoveryRunTracker state machine transitions (accepted_async → terminal/polling_error paths). The prior review for browser-run-status-tracking-and-recovery documented that DiscoveryRunTracker could not be tested in Node.js vm.runInContext due to const/class isolation issues, and a test file was removed. If deterministic automated checks for tracker transitions are still desired, they would need to be added in a different testing context (e.g., browser-based test or integration test that doesn't rely on vm.runInContext).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: grep for DiscoveryRunTracker/pollAfterMs/polling_error in tests/ finds only one reference in discovery-webhook-secret-header.test.mjs (pollAfterMs: 2000 as fixture data), not as actual tracker state machine coverage. The cold-start handoffs test (tests/discovery-cold-start-handoffs.test.mjs) tests deferral semantics, not async polling paths.

summary: Fix is correct. The accidental renaming of handleDiscoverySetupDeepLink in 0d2b092 was restored, re-establishing the cold-start discovery handoff contract. All 6 cold-start handoff tests pass. Full repo validation (272 tests) is green. One naming-collision risk and one gap in automated tracker transition coverage are documented as shared-state observations for future workers.
